Azadi Records.bio: Founded out of the street rap movement DIVINE and Naezy ignited, Azadi has built the most culturally coherent roster in Indian indie — Seedhe Maut, Prabh Deep, Ahmer, Swadesi, Tienas. Signs exclusively within Hindi hip-hop and street culture. Authenticity and scene credibility matter more than streaming numbers here.
Azadi Records.pitch_note: Not cold-pitch friendly. Build credibility in the scene first — collabs, features, shows. They'll find you.

Pagal Haina.bio: The label behind Lifafa, Peter Cat Recording Co., The F16s, and Prateek Kuhad — some of the most internationally recognised names in Indian indie. Pagal Haina is the label equivalent of an independent film house: small roster, long artist relationships, zero commercial compromise.
Pagal Haina.pitch_note: Watch how their existing artists operate — long build, deep audience, international reach. That's the benchmark they're working to.

Gully Gang Entertainment.tagline: DIVINE's label and the centre of Mumbai street hip-hop
Gully Gang Entertainment.bio: DIVINE's own imprint, built from the same streets the gully rap movement came from. Tighter and more personal than Azadi — this is DIVINE's world, and the artists on it (MC Altaf, D'Evil, Riar Saab) are part of that extended ecosystem.
Gully Gang Entertainment.pitch_note: Network-first. You need to be in the ecosystem before the label is in the picture.

DL91.bio: A Delhi-based hip-hop label and collective. Seedhe Maut are the flagship — their trajectory from underground Delhi rap to international recognition is the clearest example of what DL91 represents. Hurricane, Lil Bhavi, and GhAatak fill out a roster that consistently pushes the hardest end of the scene.
DL91.pitch_note: Scene-driven label. Proximity to Delhi hip-hop matters more than online numbers here.

ONNO Collective.tagline: Artist-run home for experimental and ambient Indian electronic music
ONNO Collective.bio: ONNO is a collective, not a label in the traditional sense — no A&R, no commercial pressure. Disco Puppet, Sawhorse, Pardafash, Catatacat make music that is genuinely difficult to place anywhere mainstream. The only Indian context for leftfield electronic and ambient work.
ONNO Collective.pitch_note: Approach as a peer, not a pitch. Collective membership is relationship-based.

Dualism Records.bio: Sahej Bakshi's own label for his Dualist Inquiry project — one of the first Indian electronic artists to build an international touring circuit. Worth knowing as a model for how an Indian electronic artist built self-sufficiency, not as a label to pitch.
Dualism Records.pitch_note: Artist-owned imprint, not taking outside submissions.

Mixtape Live.tagline: India's largest and most genre-diverse indie booking agency
Mixtape Live.bio: 27 artists across indie rock, hip-hop, math rock, electronic, and vocal music — Mixtape Live is the broadest tent in Indian indie booking. Pentagram, Skyharbor, Naezy, Voctronica. If you've built a live following and are ready for proper venue shows, this is the first agency to approach.
Mixtape Live.pitch_note: Have at least 5-10 self-booked shows documented before approaching. They need to see you can draw a room.

Krunk Live.tagline: India's premier electronic music booking agency
Krunk Live.bio: The pipeline for the club and festival electronic circuit — Anushka, Madboy/Mink, MALFNKTION, Oceantied, Sandunes. If you want to play Magnetic Fields, Vh1 Supersonic, or the major club nights, Krunk is the agency. Genre-specific and serious about production quality.
Krunk Live.pitch_note: Record your live sets well. That is the primary thing they evaluate.

MGMH (Music Gets Me High).tagline: Electronic music booking for underground and experimental acts
MGMH (Music Gets Me High).bio: MGMH books electronic artists for clubs, festivals, and headline shows. Jalebee Cartel and Nucleya are the confirmed flagship acts. Smaller and more curated than Krunk — fewer artists, longer relationships.
MGMH (Music Gets Me High).pitch_note: Smaller roster than Krunk. Approach when you already have festival credits.

REPRESENT.tagline: Mainstream-facing agency with hip-hop and EDM crossover strength
REPRESENT.bio: Works across a wide range — Armaan Malik (mainstream pop), Anuv Jain (indie folk crossover), MC Stan (street hip-hop), Lost Stories and Zaeden (EDM). Not an indie-purist agency; they work where commercial volume is. Relevant for artists building toward mainstream exposure.
REPRESENT.pitch_note: Better fit for artists with growing streaming numbers than for emerging acts.

Big Bang Music (BGBNG).tagline: Booking and management at the indie-mainstream intersection
Big Bang Music (BGBNG).bio: Shalmali Kholgade, Anumita Nadesan, Jasleen Royal — a mixed roster spanning Bollywood playback, indie pop, and crossover. BGBNG sits at the intersection of commercial and independent, working with artists who have mainstream exposure but an independent identity.
Big Bang Music (BGBNG).pitch_note: Strong existing profile is a prerequisite here.

Collective Artists Network.tagline: Premium management for India's established crossover artists
Collective Artists Network.bio: Pritam, Jasleen Royal, Shilpa Rao, Jonita Gandhi — Collective Artists manages artists who are already household names in the Bollywood-crossover space. This is not an entry-level agency. Think of it as the endgame, not the starting point.
Collective Artists Network.pitch_note: Not accessible to emerging artists. Study their roster to understand what the upper end looks like.

AMG India.tagline: Classical, jazz, and world music management
AMG India.bio: Sivamani, Louiz Banks, Kala Ramnath, Jayanthi Kumaresh — AMG India manages India's foremost classical and jazz musicians. A completely separate ecosystem from indie pop, rock, or electronic. Relevant only if you're working in classical or jazz traditions.
AMG India.pitch_note: Wrong agency for indie pop, rock, or electronic artists.

Universal Music India (VYRL).tagline: UMG's India digital label — mainstream and Bollywood-adjacent
Universal Music India (VYRL).bio: VYRL is Universal's India-specific digital label. Mithoon, Asees Kaur, Arjun Kanungo. Occasionally picks up independent artists with significant streaming numbers. Requires industry relationships or a proven track record to reach A&R.
Universal Music India (VYRL).pitch_note: Build your numbers first. Industry relationships open doors here more than cold outreach.

Sony Music India (NINE).tagline: Sony's hip-hop and urban music arm for India
Sony Music India (NINE).bio: NINE is Sony's label for Indian hip-hop and urban music — Raftaar, Talwiinder, The Kidd. The major label most aligned with the indie hip-hop scene, though it operates with major label infrastructure and commercial expectations.
Sony Music India (NINE).pitch_note: More accessible than other major arms, but still a major label deal structure.

Sony Music India (Day One).tagline: Sony's indie and alternative music imprint
Sony Music India (Day One).bio: Day One is Sony's India imprint for independent and alternative artists. Tanmaya Bhatnagar and Kevin Shaji (Pina Colada Blues) suggest a taste for quiet, indie-folk-adjacent music. Small roster — a sign they're curating carefully.
Sony Music India (Day One).pitch_note: Worth approaching if you're in the quiet-indie space and have proven an audience organically.

Warner Music India.tagline: WMG's India operation — mainstream pop and Punjabi crossover
Warner Music India.bio: Darshan Raval, King, Diljit Dosanjh. King's trajectory — from independent to Warner to global recognition — is the clearest example of what Warner India looks for: proven audience before the deal, not potential.
Warner Music India.pitch_note: Build your numbers first. Warner India responds to momentum, not cold pitches.

Speed Records.bio: The dominant force in Punjabi pop — Sidhu Moosewala (historical), Gippy Grewal, B Praak. Not the right fit for artists outside the Punjabi music ecosystem. Their scale and commercial focus are built around one specific genre and regional market.
Speed Records.pitch_note: Not relevant for indie pop, electronic, or hip-hop outside the Punjabi ecosystem.

T-Series.tagline: India's largest music label — Bollywood and mainstream
T-Series.bio: India's biggest music company by volume. Bollywood soundtracks, mainstream digital singles, established commercial names. Included here to complete the India landscape — not an indie pathway by any measure.

Saregama.tagline: India's oldest label — catalog-heavy, closed to new indie signings
Saregama.bio: Saregama holds one of the largest catalogs of Indian music — classical, film, folk. New signings are rare and tend toward established names. Primarily relevant as a licensing and distribution partner for existing catalog, not as an A&R pathway.
Saregama.pitch_note: Not an A&R pathway for new or independent artists.
